codeReview.summary: The feature substantially unifies external provider plugins under the central backend/frontend plugin registries and removes forced np: namespacing, but it introduces a blocking reserved-plugin-ID collision that can clobber builtin registration.

issues.description: External provider manifests are only regex-validated (`backend/services/node_provider_plugin_manager.py:48-52`) and can use reserved IDs such as `builtin`. Reload then unconditionally executes `unregister_plugin(manifest.id)` before registering provider executors (`backend/services/node_provider_registry.py:273-279`). A provider with id `builtin` will deregister the core builtin plugin and replace its registry ownership/metadata, breaking builtin executor/hook behavior. The frontend refresh path has the same collision surface by unregistering arbitrary provider plugin IDs (`app/lib/services/node-provider-definitions.ts:51-59`) before registering manifests (`:87-89`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Reserved plugin IDs and ownership boundaries are runtime-significant but not explicitly documented/enforced as mission-level convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Builtin manifest uses `id: 'builtin'` (`nodes/manifest.ts:131-133`), frontend plugin registry has override semantics for `dynamic` (`app/lib/flow/plugin-registry.ts:30`), while external provider manifest parsing accepts any regex match without reserved-ID denial (`backend/services/node_provider_plugin_manager.py:48-52`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation contract evidence for VAL-EXT-007 includes agent-browser screenshot, but backend-worker procedure emphasizes CLI/tests only, so workers can satisfy skill steps while missing expected evidence shape.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md` VAL-EXT-007 lists evidence as unit tests + agent-browser screenshot; `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md` procedure covers tests/grep/manual CLI checks; reviewed handoff for session `01d90dc7-4fa8-4300-a73e-2678381ad34c` reports `interactiveChecks: []`.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit diff for external-plugin-unified-api. Unified registry goals are mostly met, but there is a blocking correctness regression: external plugins can use reserved IDs (notably `builtin`) and overwrite/deregister builtin plugin registration.
